expected_behavior: Flags the plain CREATE INDEX on a large table as write-blocking and recommends CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY
Flags the single unbatched UPDATE over ~80M rows (long lock/transaction) and recommends a batched, throttled, idempotent, resumable backfill as a separate step
Discusses rolling-deploy/expand-contract discipline for the schema change rather than doing everything in one migration

scenarios.query:
Review this migration:
```ruby
class RenameUsersTable < ActiveRecord::Migration[7.1]
  def change
    rename_column :accounts, :email, :primary_email
    drop_table :user_preferences
  end
end
```
expected_behavior: Flags the in-place column rename as breaking the currently-running app version (old code still uses email during a rolling deploy)
Recommends the expand/contract rename recipe: add new column, dual-write, backfill, move reads, stop writing old, drop last
Flags drop_table as destructive DDL that must be gated until the new path is verified and old code drained (and notes reversibility)
Recognizes the nullable expand plus NOT VALID constraint (validated later) as the safe pattern
Does not demand the constraint validation, NOT NULL, or contract step happen in this migration — deferring them is the point
